top_signals.title: China's Fuel Exports to Remain Low as Curbs Persist
top_signals.summary: China's fuel exports will rise only slightly in June from May as government restrictions on shipments largely remain in place to preserve supply for the domestic market amid the biggest oil disruption in history. China is expected to export about 550,000 metric tons of fuels next month, up from around 500,000 tons set to be shipped this month, trade sources with knowledge of the plans told Reuters on Friday. Days after the conflict in the Middle East erupted and led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the Chinese government moved to ban all…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Legal disputes rip through oil shipping after Hormuz closure
top_signals.summary: TotalEnergies weighs lawsuit against Baltic Exchange, following Mercuria, as shipping derivative losses mount
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Mexico Mid-May Inflation Slowed After Banxico Ended Easing Cycle
top_signals.summary: Mexico’s annual inflation slowed in line with expectations in early May, after the central bank late last month delivered a final interest rate cut of a two-year easing cycle.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: US Blockade Is Painful for Iran, Sanders Says
top_signals.summary: Col. Wayne Sanders (Ret.), of Bloomberg Intelligence, says the US blockade of Iran is causing some pain and does give President Donald Trump some leverage. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
